pullRequest.title: [DOM-55858] Refactor to use project service instead of project persister

nodes.body: Can you use Instant? Instant is the best practice in our codebase
java.time.Instant or org.joda.time.Instance? I can replace in the ProjectSummary but that value is coming from Project which currently use java.time.DateTime.
ok no need to replace the project data type, just add it to ProjectSummary. java.time.Instant

I'm sorry you had to go through and update all of these individual instances of ProjectSummary. If you have time, you could create a shareable function for creating test data and use that instead. You could add it to this https://github.com/cerebrotech/domino/blob/develop/server/src/tests-lib/scala/domino/server/util/TestUtils.scala

Using that class would be tricky because there is an existing method for creating a project summary, so the import conflict and there are repeated import although are not the same (just same class name - using import alias solve that but at least tricky), there is a method like the one you propose in "WorkspaceTestUtil" I can create a file in the domino/server/util like ProjectTestUtils and move it there and try to use from other project, but not sure if that is class is reachable from all the projects
added object ProjectTestUtils, let me know if that is ok
What should be added to project summary so tht you don't need to use project persister anymore?

the audit report shows dataset permission changes, so it is fetching projects associated with dataset, so not sure if using project service will allow fetching that data based on principal, another option is using a servicie principal but also not sure if that can allow the fetching

got it. I guess we should ultimately ask product if a principal who isn't allowed to see a project that a dataset is attached to should be allowed to see details about the project.

If product says we shouldn't show that information, then maybe in the case that a user can't see details about the project, we could show a string "redacted".
spoke with luis on slack: apparently pms are ok with this
